reasoning: An Elo gain of 1% from 2742 requires gaining over 27 rating points. For an elite player like Shakhriyar Mamedyarov, who has historically established ratings and plays mostly top-tier opposition, gaining 27 points in a single month or even three months involves scoring massively above expected outcomes (e.g., +5 or +6 in a single elite tournament or consistently overperforming across multiple events). This sort of point farm is extraordinarily rare — confirming the stated empirical base rate of 0.007 for such leaps. Furthermore, the first time horizon is only 7 days (Nov 9 to Nov 16). Given that FIDE only issues official rating updates on the 1st of each month, the number on Wikipedia's 'FIDE rankings' page almost certainly will not change between Nov 9 and Nov 16. Even for the Dec 9 and Feb 7 dates, the base rate for an established former >2800 player making such a rapid leap at this late stage in his career is firmly anchored near zero. To satisfy lower bound probability rules, the estimates are placed at the minimum allowable limits, increasing slightly with the horizon.
